questions.text: Where did Bepin Choudhury go every Monday after work?
questions.explanation: He went to Kalicharan's in New Market to buy books.

questions.text: What kind of books did Bepin Babu usually buy?
questions.explanation: He was a serious reader who preferred thrillers and mysteries.

questions.text: Who was the man who approached Bepin Babu at the book shop?
questions.explanation: Parimal Ghose was the stranger who claimed to have met Bepin in Ranchi.

questions.text: In which year did the stranger claim Bepin Babu visited Ranchi?
questions.explanation: The man insisted the trip took place in '58.

questions.text: Where did Bepin Babu believe he was during October 1958?
options: In Kanpur at a friend's place
questions.explanation: He was sure he had spent the Puja holidays at Haridas Bagchi's place in Kanpur.

questions.text: What personal details did Parimal Ghose know about Bepin Babu?
questions.explanation: The stranger knew intimate details, which convinced Bepin that the man wasn't just guessing.

questions.text: According to the stranger, what injury did Bepin get in Ranchi?
options: A cut on his right knee at Hudroo Falls
questions.explanation: The stranger used this specific detail to prove they had met.

questions.text: Who did the stranger say was also in Ranchi at the same time?
questions.explanation: Dinesh Mukerji was mentioned as the person Bepin stayed with (or near).

questions.text: Why couldn't Bepin Babu write to Haridas Bagchi to confirm his stay in Kanpur?
options: Haridas had left for Japan with his wife
questions.explanation: Bepin remembered that Haridas was no longer in Kanpur.

questions.text: What did Bepin Babu do to check for the physical evidence of the Ranchi trip?
options: He rolled up his trousers to check his right knee
questions.explanation: He found a mark of an old cut, which confused him further.

questions.text: When Bepin Babu called Dinesh Mukerji, what was the reply?
options: Dinesh said they never went to Ranchi
Dinesh confirmed they were both in Ranchi in '58
questions.explanation: Dinesh's confirmation made Bepin very worried about his mental health.

questions.text: Who was Chunilal?
options: An old schoolmate of Bepin Babu
questions.explanation: Chunilal had been coming to see Bepin for help in finding a job.

questions.text: What did Chunilal say when Bepin asked him about the 1958 trip?
options: He confirmed Bepin went to Ranchi and that he himself booked the ticket
He said Bepin went to Delhi
questions.explanation: Chunilal's confirmation was the third 'proof' of the trip.

questions.text: What was the name of the doctor Bepin Babu consulted?
questions.explanation: Dr. Chanda was a young physician with bright eyes and a sharp nose.

questions.text: What was unique about Bepin Babu's case according to the doctor?
options: He had forgotten only one specific episode of his life
questions.explanation: The doctor found it strange that Bepin remembered everything except the Ranchi trip.

questions.text: What did Dr. Chanda suggest Bepin Babu should do?
options: Go to Ranchi to see the places again
questions.explanation: He thought visiting the site might trigger his memory.

questions.text: How did Bepin Babu feel when he arrived at Ranchi station?
options: He felt sure he had never been there before
questions.explanation: He didn't recognize any of the streets or buildings.

questions.text: What happened to Bepin Babu at Hudroo Falls?
options: He fell unconscious and was found by two Gujaratis
questions.explanation: He was found lying next to a boulder by a group of picnickers.

questions.text: When Bepin Babu returned to Calcutta, what did he receive?
questions.explanation: The letter revealed the truth about the whole situation.

questions.text: What was the actual reason for the 'Ranchi trip' stories?
options: It was a practical joke played by Chunilal
questions.explanation: Chunilal played the trick to take revenge for Bepin's lack of help during his hard times.

questions.text: How did Chunilal feel about Bepin Babu's behavior?
options: He was hurt that his old friend didn't help him find a job
questions.explanation: He wanted to show Bepin that 'imagination' and friendship are more important than money.

questions.text: What did Bepin Babu tell Dr. Chanda when he came for a follow-up?
options: That he remembered everything as soon as he reached Ranchi
That he was going to Ranchi again
questions.explanation: He lied to save face, even though he now knew the truth was a prank.

questions.text: Bepin Babu lived alone and was not a 'good mixer'. This means:
options: He was not very social and didn't have many friends
questions.explanation: The story describes him as a lonely person who spent most of his time reading.

questions.text: What was Bepin Babu's biggest fear during the story?
options: Going insane or losing his mind
questions.explanation: The idea of a memory gap made him doubt his sanity.

questions.text: Who is the author of 'Bepin Choudhury’s Lapse of Memory'?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray was a famous Indian filmmaker and writer.

questions.text: Where did Bepin Babu work?
options: In a big firm where he had a responsible job
questions.explanation: He had been working in the same firm for twenty-five years.

questions.text: Bepin Babu's brother had died of:
questions.explanation: This was the reason Bepin didn't want to visit the mental hospital.

questions.text: What did Parimal Ghose say Bepin Babu always carried during his sightseeing?
options: A bag with his books in it
questions.explanation: Bepin's love for books was a well-known trait used by Parimal to sound authentic.

questions.text: The story takes place in which Indian city (Bepin's home)?
questions.explanation: Bepin lived in Calcutta and travelled to Ranchi to check his memory.

questions.text: In the end, Bepin Babu asked the doctor for a painkiller for his ________.
questions.explanation: He claimed he had a pain in his hip due to a fall in Ranchi.

questions.text: What was the color of the envelope that Chunilal sent?
questions.explanation: The green envelope was left on his desk by the servant.

questions.text: The phrase 'losing one's mind' means:
questions.explanation: Bepin feared he was becoming 'mad'.

questions.text: How long had Bepin Babu been working in his firm?
questions.explanation: His long tenure showed he was a stable and reliable employee.

questions.text: What did Dinesh Mukerji say he had been to Ranchi twice in '58?
questions.explanation: Dinesh recalled a wedding in February and the trip with Bepin in October.

questions.text: The main theme of the story is:
options: A humorous prank and the trickery of memory
questions.explanation: It's a psychological comedy about a man being tricked by his friend.

questions.text: Why did Bepin Babu avoid meeting Chunilal initially?
options: He couldn't help him with a job and found his visits annoying
questions.explanation: Bepin found Chunilal's persistent requests for work 'undesirable'.

questions.text: What does Bepin's reaction to the prank show about him?
options: He is easily worried and values his reputation/sanity
He has a great sense of humor
questions.explanation: He becomes extremely distressed over a small memory gap.

questions.text: Chunilal's letter said Bepin was 'affluent'. What does affluent mean?
questions.explanation: Chunilal contrasted Bepin's wealth with his own poverty.

questions.text: What was the 'imagination' Chunilal mentioned in his letter?
options: His own ability to create a convincing prank
questions.explanation: He used his creative mind to punish Bepin for his coldness.

questions.text: By the end of the story, Bepin Babu is:
options: Recovered from his fear but perhaps embarrassed
questions.explanation: The realization that it was a prank ended his medical anxiety.
